title: Identify the estimator and target parameter
text: The estimator is \alpha_hat=(1/100)*sum{i\=1}{100}{X_i}, and it is trying to estimate the parameter $$\alpha=E[X_i]$$. Thus, \alpha is the true population mean number of games attended (parameter), while $$a_{hat}$$ is the sample mean computed from the $$100$$ observed students.

title: Understanding Bias
text: The bias of an estimator tells us whether, on average, the estimator overestimates or underestimates the true parameter. It is defined as Bias(\alpha_hat)=E[\alpha_hat]-\alpha. If the bias is $$0$$, the estimator is called unbiased.

title: Compute $$E[a_hat]$$ first
text: Substitute the given estimator into the expectation: E[a_hat]=E[(1/100)*sum{i\=1}{100}{X_i}]

title: Using Linearity of Expectation
text: Linearity of expectation means $$E[A+B]=E[A]+E[B]+E[A]+E[B]$$ and $$E[cA]=cE[A]$$ where c is a constant. Independence is NOT a required condition for this property to work. Use this property to move the expectation inside the sum: E[(1/100)*sum{i\=1}{100}{X_i}]=(1/100)*sum{i\=1}{100}{E[X_i]}

title: Identically Distributed
text: Because the $$X_i$$ are identically distributed, they all have the same probability distribution and, thus, have the same expected value. Since the problem defines $$\alpha=E[X_i]$$, we can replace each $$E[X_i]$$ by \alpha: (1/100)*sum{i\=1}{100}{E[X_i]}=(1/100)*sum{i\=1}{100}{\alpha}

title: Simplify the Sum
text: There are $$100$$ terms in the sum, and each term is \alpha. What is the simplified expression of (1/100)*sum{i\=1}{100}{\alpha}?

title: Compute Bias
text: We know that $$E[lpha_hat]=\alpha$$. Plug this into the bias formula: Bias(\alpha_hat)=E[\alpha_hat]-\alpha=\alpha - $$\alpha=0$$. Thus, the estimator is unbiased.

title: Understanding Unbiased Estimator
text: An estimator is unbiased if its expected value equals the true parameter: $$E[lpha_hat]=\alpha$$. This does not mean every sample gives the exact true value. It means that over many repeated random samples, the estimator is centered at the truth on average.

title: Concept Check: parameter vs estimator
text: The parameter \alpha is a fixed but unknown population value. The estimator \alpha_hat depends on the random sample, so it changes from sample to sample.
